root_text.an5.67:1.1: “Yo hi koci, bhikkhave, bhikkhu vā bhikkhunī vā pañca dhamme bhāveti, pañca dhamme bahulīkaroti, tassa dvinnaṁ phalānaṁ aññataraṁ phalaṁ pāṭikaṅkhaṁ—
root_text.an5.67:1.2: diṭṭheva dhamme aññā, sati vā upādisese anāgāmitā.
root_text.an5.67:2.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu chandasamādhipadhānasaṅkhārasamannāgataṁ iddhipādaṁ bhāveti,
root_text.an5.67:2.7: Yo hi koci, bhikkhave, bhikkhu vā bhikkhunī vā ime pañca dhamme bhāveti, ime pañca dhamme bahulīkaroti, tassa dvinnaṁ phalānaṁ aññataraṁ phalaṁ pāṭikaṅkhaṁ—
root_text.an5.67:2.8: diṭṭheva dhamme aññā, sati vā upādisese anāgāmitā”ti.

translation_text.an5.67:1.1: “Mendicants, any monk or nun who develops and cultivates five qualities can expect one of two results:
translation_text.an5.67:1.2: enlightenment in this very life, or if there’s something left over, non-return.
translation_text.an5.67:2.2: A mendicant develops the basis of psychic power that has immersion due to enthusiasm, and active effort …
translation_text.an5.67:2.3: A mendicant develops the basis of psychic power that has immersion due to energy, and active effort …
translation_text.an5.67:2.4: A mendicant develops the basis of psychic power that has immersion due to mental development, and active effort …
translation_text.an5.67:2.5: A mendicant develops the basis of psychic power that has immersion due to inquiry, and active effort.
translation_text.an5.67:2.6: And the fifth is sheer vigor.
translation_text.an5.67:2.7: Any monk or nun who develops and cultivates these five qualities can expect one of two results:
translation_text.an5.67:2.8: enlightenment in this very life, or if there’s something left over, non-return.”

variant_text.an5.67:1.1: pañca dhamme → ime pañca dhamme (mr) | bhāveti, pañca dhamme → ime pañca dhamme (mr)
